fields.description:
## Description

**What**: Update all project documentation that references the removed enforcement hooks, two-layer review gate, direct-push merge flow, and per-epic (vs. per-batch) sprint merge model.
**Why**: Stale documentation referencing non-existent gates or the wrong sprint merge cadence will cause agents to follow wrong instructions and developers to expect enforcement or behavior that no longer exists.
**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md Critical Rules (rules 12, 13, 22, 25 — rewrite for PR-based enforcement model), CLAUDE.md architecture section (remove hook descriptions, update commit workflow reference), REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md (remove review-status gate references), per-worktree-review-commit.md (remove record-status step references), health-check.sh (update or remove review-status scan), sprint SKILL.md (update review-status, merge-to-main references, AND document the new per-batch PR-merge cadence — replace "merge at epic close" language with "merge per batch, epic completion is final batch's PR merge"), COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md (PR-based flow + Jira-Ticket/DSO-Task trailer behavior), KNOWN-ISSUES.md
- OUT: Creating new documentation files, Epic 3aaa-0238 (CI pipeline) documentation, sprint orchestrator code changes (87ab-a487)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Critical Rules section accurately reflects the PR-based enforcement model: rules 12, 13, 22, 25 rewritten to remove references to two-layer review gate, pre-commit hooks, review-status files, and local bypass detection
  ← Satisfies SC5: "COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md updated to reflect simplified local workflow"
- When this story is complete, REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md, per-worktree-review-commit.md, health-check.sh no longer reference removed gates or artifacts
  ← Satisfies SC5
- When this story is complete, sprint SKILL.md documents the per-batch PR-merge cadence (PR per batch, wait on CI green, next batch starts after merge, epic completion is final batch's PR merge) — no remaining "merge at epic close" or cumulative-push language
  ← Satisfies SC9 (documentation portion; behavior change owned by 87ab-a487)
- When this story is complete, COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md documents the Jira-Ticket and DSO-Task git trailer behavior introduced by cab1-600f — which fields populate each trailer, that trailers are omitted (not errored) when source values are unavailable, and that the blank-line separator is added automatically by git interpret-trailers
  ← Satisfies SC7 (documentation portion)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
